paper_threads.summary: Discussion of a new paper called 'Intern-S1-Pro: Scientific Multimodal Foundation Model at Trillion Scale' - appears to be a large-scale scientific AI model.
Paper on AI sycophancy published in Science, showing that discussing interpersonal conflicts with AI systems can have negative effects on prosocial intentions.
Discussion of research on how subliminal prompting propagates across networks of interacting AI agents, raising safety concerns.
Paper discussion about whether the Internet is being rewritten to serve AI agents and how to measure this phenomenon.

announcements.summary: Meta releases SAM 3.1, an update to their segmentation model that introduces object multiplexing to improve video processing capabilities.
Nous Research announces Hermes Agent v0.5.0 release with various updates and improvements to their AI agent framework.
Release of slime v0.2.4 focusing on profiling and observability features including rollout trace timeline viewer and real-time metrics reporting.
Nintendo Switch 2 and Mario Kart Bundle available for $399/$439 on Woot, offering both physical and digital copy options.
Microsoft announces partnership with Crusoe on a 900MW AI factory in Abilene, Texas for large-scale AI infrastructure.

discussions.summary: François Chollet discusses intelligence as a conversion ratio with optimality bounds, arguing against the notion of unbounded scalar intelligence like '10,000 IQ'.
Discussion about whether Gaussian splats should be considered 'AI' and the problems of using 'AI' to refer to heterogeneous technologies.
Debate about automating white collar work, with skepticism about claims that we're months away from full automation based on current AI capabilities.
Discussion of intelligence and its potential bounds, comparing human and AI intelligence development with analogies to chess and alien encounters.
Analysis of China's manufacturing dominance and discussion of economic/technological competition between nations, particularly in AI development.
